properties.publicTransport: Buses will be diverting while roads are closed. Plan your trip at transportnsw.info.
properties.otherAdvice:
Up to 9,500 participants are expected to take part in this year’s Harbour 10 event, formerly known as Sydney Harbour 10k & 5k.

The course starts on George Street in Circular Quay from 7.30am and ends at the Overseas Passenger Terminal by 10.30am. All event festivities will be complete by 1pm. 

The course travels around the harbour foreshore using on and off-road pathways via Dawes Point, Millers Point, Barangaroo, King St Wharf, Darling Harbour and Pyrmont.

Road closures will be in place to safely facilitate the event. Motorists should allow extra travel time and follow directions of traffic controllers on site.

Key road closures:

  • Circular Quay West – 4am to 1pm: at the Overseas Passenger Terminal (OPT), from Argyle Street to the roundabout.
  • Sydney CBD & The Rocks – 4.30am to 11am: George Street from Alfred Street to Hickson Road; Hickson Road from George Street to Pottinger Street; and the OPT Upper Roadway.
  • The Rocks – 6am to 11am: Hickson Road from Barton Street and Pottinger Street; Napoleon Street from Kent Street to Sussex Street; and sections of Dalgety Road and Towns Place.
  • Dawes Point, Millers Point & Barangaroo – 6am to 10am: Sections of Hickson Road, Barton Street, Slip Street and Sussex Street.
  • Pyrmont – 7.30am to 10am: Sections of Murray Street, Pirrama Road and the Wharf 7 & 10 access road.
  • Changed access – King St Wharf and Barangaroo South - 8:15am to 9:30am: due to the closure of Sussex St, there is no exit from King St Wharf/Barangaroo South between 8:15am and 9.30am. Entry via Harbour St and Wheat Rd only. Roads affected include Barangaroo Ave, Cuthbert St, Erskine St, King St, Lime St, Shelley St and Watermans Quay.

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip. The Hordern Pavilion is easily accessible by light rail and buses.
properties.otherAdvice:
City2Surf Expo at the Hordern Pavilion in Moore Park from Thursday 6 to Saturday 8 August.

  • Thursday 6 August: 12pm to 7pm
  • Friday 7 August: 8am to 6:30pm
  • Saturday 8 August: 7:30am to 4:30pm

Large crowds are expected to attend the Expo.

SATURDAY 8 AUGUST: the Moore Park precinct will be extremely busy while large crowds attend other events at nearby venues. Keep this in mind when planning your trip as significant traffic delays are expected, parking will be in high demand and transport services will be busy.

Motorists should allow extra travel time on roads including the Eastern Distributor, Moore Park Rd, Cleveland St, Anzac Pde and Lang Rd.

Attendees are urged to catch public transport and leave the car at home to avoid the traffic delays and high demand for parking

roads.mainStreet: Moore Park Road, Anzac Parade, Lang Road, Eastern Distributor

properties.publicTransport: Buses and light rail services run regularly to Rozelle. Metro trackwork may affect your travel on both weekends. Plan your trip at transportnsw.info
properties.otherAdvice:
Upcoming events at White Bay Power Station on Saturday 18, Friday 24 & Saturday 25 July

  • Ministry of Sound: Testament 90s - Saturday 18 July 4pm to midnight
  • Ministry of Sound: Testament 00s - Friday 24 and Saturday 25 July 4pm to midnight nightly

Traffic will be heavy on roads approaching White Bay Power Station as large crowds travel to and from the event.

Parking in the area is very limited, attendees are encouraged to catch public transport.

Motorists should allow extra travel time on Victoria Rd, the City West Link and Robert St.

properties.publicTransport: Travel on public transport is included for participants and volunteers. Buses between the city and the Eastern Suburbs will experience significant impacts while roads are closed – some services will not run until roads have reopened; some services will divert and use different stops. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Extensive road closures and widespread clearway parking restrictions will be in place from the Sydney CBD to Bondi Beach on Sunday 9 August while 90k people take part in the annual City2Surf fun run.  

Roads around the start area at Hyde Park will start closing from 3.30am while major roads along the course will close from 6am. Roads will progressively reopen from 11.30am in the city, 12pm in Edgecliff, 1pm in Rose Bay, 2pm in Vaucluse and Dover Heights, and 4pm at Bondi Beach.  

Motorists are encouraged to avoid non-essential travel in the areas surrounding the event while roads are closed. If you must drive, diversions will be in place and heavy traffic is expected – allow extra trave time and follow detour signs. 

Major closures – Sunday 9 August: 

  • Sydney CBD – 3.30am to 12pm: sections of College Street, Macquarie Street, Prince Albert Road, St James Road, and other surrounding streets until 11.30am, and sections of Park Street and William Street until 12pm. 
  • Woolloomooloo to Edgecliff – 6am to 12pm: William Street from the city through to New South Head Road at Ocean Avenue, and sections of Bayswater Road, New Beach Road and Ocean Street. No access to the city via New South Head Road west of Ocean Street until 12pm; use the alternative route via Oxford Street. 
  • Cross City Tunnel closed eastbound – 6am to 12pm: westbound access to the Cross City Tunnel and Craigend Street is maintained via McLachlan Avenue at Rushcutters Bay. From the Eastern Distributor: there is no access to William Street or to the westbound lanes of the Cross City Tunnel. 
  • Edgecliff to Rose Bay – 7am to 1pm: New South Head Road from Ocean Street/Avenue to Dover Road, and sections of Balfour Road, O’Sullivan Road and Wolseley Road.  
  • Rose Bay to North Bondi – 7am to 2pm: New South Head Road from Dover Road to Old South Head Road, Old South Head Road from New South Head Road to Oceanview Avenue, Military Road from Old South Head Road to Blair Street, and sections of Blair Street and Oceanview Avenue. Military Road from Lancaster Road to Blake Street will be closed from 5am, along with the eastbound lanes from Old South Head Road to Wilfield Avenue. 
  • Bondi Beach – 6am and 4pm: Campbell Parade – from Blair Street to Hall Street from 6am and from Hall Street to Francis Street from 7am, Military Road from Blair Street to Hastings Parade, and sections of Beach Road, Curlewis Street and Lamrock Avenue. Sections of Bondi Road, Wairoa Avenue, Hastings Parade, Francis Street and other local streets will also be closed from 7am.  
  • Bondi Junction – 9am to 4pm: sections of Bronte Road, Grafton Street, Grosvenor Street and Oxford Street around Bondi Junction. 
  • Queen Elizabeth Drive (Bondi Beach) from Saturday 8 August: there will be restricted access from 1am to 1pm on Saturday 8 August, followed by a full closure from 1pm Saturday 8 August until 4pm Sunday 9 August.  

Crossover points along the course will be maintained for local access until 7am or 7.10am, depending on the location. After these times, there will be no vehicle access permitted while the run is underway and until roads have safely reopened to traffic. 

Special event clearway parking restrictions will be in effect along both sides of the entire 14km course and some surrounding streets between 1am to 4pm on event day. Clearways between the city and Rose Bay will lift by 12pm. A clearway will also be in place on the eastern side of O’Sullivan Road in Road Bay from 11pm Saturday 8 until 3pm Sunday 9 August.  

Special event clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

arrangementElements.title: Special Event Clearways

arrangementElements.html:
VEHICLE CROSSOVER POINTS

Crossover points along the course will be maintained for local access until 7am or 7.10am, depending on location. There will be NO vehicle access after this time / while the run is underway until roads have safely reopened to traffic.  

Residents located between the course and the coastline who need to drive should relocate their vehicle to the other side of the course before roads close then cross the course by foot prior to the event starting. 

 

Kings Cross

  • Precinct access is maintained via Darlinghurst Road, Victoria Street and Ward Avenue. 

William Street

  • Woolloomooloo / Darlinghurst: two-way crossover on Crown Street until 7.10am.  
  • Elizabeth Bay / Darlinghurst: two-way crossover from Victoria Street to Darlinghurst Road maintained throughout the event. 

New South Head Road

  • Darling Point: Southbound exit only from New Beach Road to Neild Avenue until 7am. No entry after 7am. 
  • Double Bay to Edgecliff: Southbound exit only from Ocean Avenue to Ocean Street until 7.10am. No entry after 7am. 
  • Double Bay to Bellevue Hill: Southbound exit only from Cross Street to Bellevue Road until 7.10am. No entry after 7am. 
  • Vaucluse/Rose Bay: Vaucluse Road to Towns Road two-way crossover until 7.10am. No entry after 7am. 
  • Vaucluse: Southbound exit only onto Old South Head Road at New South Head Road until 7.10am. No entry after 7am. 

Campbell Parade (North Bondi

  • One-way westbound exit only from Brighton Boulevard and Ramsgate Avenue until 7am. No entry after 6am. 

LOCAL ACCESS 

  • Bondi Road: Local residents are strongly encouraged to avoid unnecessary travel on Bondi Road – it will be heavily congested with hundreds of buses transporting thousands of participants between the Bondi Beach and Bondi Junction. Residents north of Bondi Road should use Old South Head Road to enter/exit the area via Blair Street. Residents south of Bondi Road should use Birrell Street. 
  • Bondi Beach: Local access for properties at Bondi Beach will be maintained via one-way traffic only – entry available via Curlewis Street, Gould Street and Hall Street. Properties located between Lamrock Avenue and Hall Street can exit via Hall Street only; no access to or from Lamrock Avenue. 
  • Dover Heights: Managed access for properties on Military Road between Lancaster Road and Blake Street from 5am to 7am only. 
  • Cross City Tunnel westbound: Westbound access to Cross City Tunnel and Craigend Street is maintained via McLachlan Avenue at Rushcutters Bay. The tunnel will be closed eastbound until 12pm.
arrangementElements.title: Vehicle Crossover Points & Local Access Changes

arrangementElements.html:
Taxi Rank change in Bondi Junction

Due to road closures, the taxi rank on Grosvenor Street in Bondi Junction will be relocated to the western side of Bronte Road between Gray Street and Spring Street. An additional taxi zone will also be available on the western side of Bronte Road, opposite Allens Parade.

Dedicated rideshare pick-up

A dedicated rideshare pick-up area will be located on Gould Street between Warners Avenue and Beach Road at Bondi Beach. Access to rideshare will be available on both sides of the road. To reduce congestion and wait times, please only request rideshare once you are ready to leave and have arrived at the pick-up location. 

Exclusion zones and geo-fence restrictions – Bondi Beach

  • Sydney CBD to Bondi Beach / Bondi Junction: all closed roads.
  • Bondi Road: No eastbound/westbound travel between Council Street and Dudley Street from 9am to 4pm. Travel via Birrell Street and Old South Head Road instead.
  • Francis Street: southeast of Lucius Street
  • Lamrock Avenue: southeast of Sir Thomas Mitchell Road
  • Curlewis, O’Brien and Roscoe streets: east of Glenayr Avenue
  • Ramsgate Avenue: southeast of Gould Street
  • Gould Street: southwest of Curlewis Street
  • Warners Avenue: southeast of Gould Street
  • Consett, Chambers and Jaques avenues: between Hall Street and Lamrock Avenue

Accessible drop-off and pick-up locations

Wheelchair-accessible taxis are not permitted to enter closed roads. Drivers are reminded to pick-up / drop-off passengers outside of exclusion zones and away from closed roads.

Drop-off in Sydney CBD

The accessible drop-off for the Start area is the western side of Phillip Street, between Martin Place and Hunter Street. Phillip Street access is via King Street only. Phillip Street is closed southbound from Hunter Street to create a one-way northbound entry and exit system.

Pick-up from Bondi Beach

The accessible pick-up location is on the southern side of Warners Avenue, between Wairoa Avenue and the mid-block pedestrian crossing behind Bondi Beach Public School. Approach eastbound via Ramsgate Avenue then turn right into Wairoa Avenue and right into Warners Avenue. Participants are asked to be on-site and ready to depart before vehicles approach for pick-up.

ASAP Australia at TikTok Entertainment Centre on Saturday 25 July. Shows start at 10:30am & 6pm.

Traffic may be heavier than usual as attendees travel to and from the event.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Eels v Panthers at CommBank Stadium in Parramatta. Kick off at 7.50pm, full time at 9.35pm.

Traffic will be heavy on roads approaching Parramatta and nearby car parks are expected to be in high demand.

Allow extra travel time on roads including Parramatta Road, Church Street, Victoria Road, James Ruse Drive and Pennant Hills Road. 

Motorists should also exercise caution as pedestrian activity will be high on roads between Parramatta Interchange and the stadium. 

Drivers are reminded that light rail services are running through Parramatta CBD. Trams can’t stop quickly or easily – so exercise extra caution when driving in the area. 

Road closures 

Roads around the stadium will close from 9.35pm until 10.05pm approx. to allow crowds to safely leave the venue. Roads will re-open once crowds have cleared the area. 

  • O’Connell Street from Grose Street to George Street 
  • Ross Street from O’Connell Street to Trott Street 
  • Victoria Road from O’Connell Street to Villiers Street 

While closures are in place motorists are encouraged to avoid driving through Parramatta CBD and detour around the closures via Grose Street, Brickfield Street, Victoria Road and Wilde Avenue in both directions.

roads.mainStreet: O'Connell Street

properties.publicTransport: Match tickets include travel on stadium shuttle buses. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL Knights v Roosters at McDonald Jones Stadium in Broadmeadow. Kick-off at 6pm and full time around 8pm.

Traffic will be heavier than usual as fans travel to and from the match. Allow extra travel time on Turton Rd, Griffiths Rd, Newcastle Rd, Lambton Rd and Bridges Rd through Broadmeadow while fans are making their way to and from the stadium.

For passenger drop-off, use the allocated 10-minute loading zone in the McDonald Jones Stadium car park. Entry is via the driveway off Turton Road.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip

Industry Celebration Day at Royal Randwick Racecourse. First race from 11.20am, last race from 4.45pm.

Traffic will be heavier than usual on Anzac Pde, Doncaster Ave, High St, Alison Rd and Darley Rd as crowds travel to and from the event.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

NRL: Dragons v Titans at St George Venues Jubilee Stadium in Kogarah. Kick-off at 2pm, full time at 3.40pm.

Traffic will be heavier than usual in both directions on the Princes Hwy approaching Kogarah as fans travel to and from the stadium.

Local roads around the stadium may close ahead of match start including Jubilee Ave, Park St and English St. Roads will re-open once crowds have cleared the area.

Fans driving to the game should allow extra time for parking and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

properties.publicTransport: Additional route 199 buses will also run towards Manly and Mona Vale after the game to help you get home. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.

NRL: Sea Eagles v Sharks at 4 Pines Park in Brookvale. NRL kick-off at 4.05pm and full time around 5.40pm.

Traffic will be heavier than usual as fans travel to and from the match. Allow extra travel time on roads approaching the stadium, including Pittwater Rd, Old Pittwater Rd, Warringah Rd and Beacon Hill Rd.

Motorists should plan to arrive early as parking will be in high demand and also be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

NOTE: There is no parking on the southern side of Federal Pde between Pine Ave and Alfred Rd permitted from 8pm the night before the match until the crowd has safely cleared the stadium after full time on game day.

NRL: Rabbitohs v Storm at Accor Stadium in Olympic Park. Kick-off at 8pm, full time around 9.45pm.

Traffic will be heavy as fans travel to the match in the busy evening peak. Motorists should allow extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

If you need to drive, pre-book parking online in advance to secure a space. Follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.

Sydney Olympic Park Authority may close some local roads to accommodate increased public transport services and ensure pedestrian safety during events. For road closure details, visit the Sydney Olympic Park interactive map for more details.

Sydney Olympic Park will be busy on Saturday 25 July with AFL and NRL matches taking place within the park and around 40,000 fans expected:

  • NRL Bulldogs Double Header at Accor Stadium. NRLW kick off at 3.15pm, NRL kick off at 5.30pm, full time at 7.15pm
  • AFL GWS Giants v Sydney Swans at ENGIE Stadium. First bounce at 4.35pm, full time at 6pm

Traffic will be heavy as fans travel to and from the matches. Motorists should allow extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

If you need to drive, pre-book parking online in advance to secure a space. Follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.

Sydney Olympic Park Authority may close some local roads to accommodate increased public transport services and ensure pedestrian safety during events. For road closure details, visit the Sydney Olympic Park interactive map for more details.

properties.publicTransport: Buses will be diverting while roads are closed for the Beach2Beach fun run. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Up to 5,500 participants will take part in the annual Beach2Beach Charity Fun Run and Festival from Dee Why to Newport on Sunday 23 August. Events start from 6.30am and will finish at around 12.30pm at Newport Beach.

Road closures will be in place between 5.30am and 12.30pm along the course to safely facilitate the event. Access for local residents will be maintained when safe to do so after runners have safely passed by along the course, and roads will start to progressively reopen from 7.30am.

  • Dee Why/Freshwater between 5.30am and 8.30am: From 5.30am, sections of Boronia Street, Griffin Road, Howard Avenue, The Strand and Pacific Parade will be closed until 7.30am; and sections of Carrington Parade, Lumsdaine Drive, Griffin Road, Headland Road, Ozone Parade and other local streets will be closed until 8.30am. Between 6.20am and 8.30am sections of Oaks Avenue will be closed.
  • Collaroy from 6am to 9.30am: Sections of Anzac Avenue, Beach Road and Florence Avenue will be closed.
  • Narrabeen/Warriewood from 6.30am to 10.30am: Narrabeen Park Parade southbound lanes will be closed between Cook Terrace (Mona Vale) and Sydney Road. One-way traffic northbound only will be maintained. Narrabeen Park Parade southbound lanes will be closed between Sydney Road and Malcolm Street. One-way traffic northbound only will be maintained. Peal Place will be under managed access for residents via Narrabeen Park Parade northbound. Sections of Sydney Road and Melbourne Avenue will also be closed.  
  • Mona Vale between 7am and 12:30pm: Grandview Parade between Barrenjoey Road and Orana Road will be closed southbound from 7am until 12:30pm, while sections of Surfview Road, Orana Road and Bassett Street will be closed between 7am and 11am.  
  • Newport from 7.15am to 12.30pm: Sections of Calvert Parade, Ross Street, Palm Road, Myola Road and The Boulevard around the beach will be closed. Managed access will be in place for residents.

Southbound lane closures and a reduced speed limit of 40km/h will also be in place on sections of Barrenjoey Road and Pittwater Road while the event takes place.

Special event clearways will be in place from 4am to 12:30pm on the eastern side of Barrenjoey Road between Palm Road (Newport) and Grandview Parade (Mona Vale), from 4am to 10am on the eastern side of Pittwater Road between Anzac Avenue (Collaroy) and 50m north of Hawkesbury Avenue (Dee Why), and from 4am to 10:30am on the eastern side of Pittwater Road between Devitt Street (Narrabeen) and Alexander Street (Collaroy).

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

arrangementElements.html:
Road and cycleway closures affect both directions unless stated otherwise. Access for local residents will be maintained when safe to do so after runners have safely passed by along the course.

Roads and cycleways will be progressively reopened from 7.30am, after cleaning.

Spence Jr v Tszyu at Afterpay Arena (formerly Qudos Bank Arena). Matches start at 9am, finish by 3pm

Traffic delays are expected as attendees make their way to Olympic Park. Motorists should allow extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

If you're driving, pre-book parking online in advance to secure a space, available until 11:59pm the night before the event unless sold out prior. If you have pre-booked your parking, follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.

Sydney Olympic Park Authority may close some local roads to accommodate increased public transport services and ensure pedestrian safety during events. For road closure details, visit the Sydney Olympic Park interactive map for more details.

NRL: Dragons v Dolphins at WIN Stadium. Kick-off at 6pm, full time around 8pm.

Traffic will be heavier than usual on roads approaching the Wollongong CBD and on local roads as fans travel to and from the match.

Local roads around the stadium may close before, during and after the show for the safety of pedestrians.

Fans driving to the game on the freeway are encouraged to use the Masters Rd exit to avoid congestion at Mount Ousley Rd, both in and out of Wollongong.

Motorists should allow extra time for parking and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

NRL: Sharks v Rabbitohs at Ocean Protect Stadium in Woolooware. Jersey Flegg kick off from 11.35am. NRL kick-off at 2pm, full time 4pm approx.

Traffic on roads approaching the stadium will be heavier than usual as fans travel to and from the match. Allow extra travel time on Captain Cook Dr, Woolooware Rd and Gannons Rd.

Captain Cook Drive will be closed in the immediate vicinity of the Stadium from 15-minutes before full-time until 30-minutes after full time (45-minute closure). 

All road closures will be implemented and managed by NSW Police. Allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity in the area.

Allow extra travel time on roads including Parramatta Road, Church Street, Victoria Road, James Ruse Drive and Pennant Hills Road. 

Motorists should also exercise caution as pedestrian activity will be high on roads between Parramatta Interchange and the stadium. 

Drivers are reminded that light rail services are running through Parramatta CBD. Trams can’t stop quickly or easily – so exercise extra caution when driving in the area. 

Road closures 

Roads around the stadium will close from 5.50pm until 6.20pm approx. to allow crowds to safely leave the venue. Roads will re-open once crowds have cleared the area. 

  • O’Connell Street from Grose Street to George Street 
  • Ross Street from O’Connell Street to Trott Street 
  • Victoria Road from O’Connell Street to Villiers Street

Motorists should allow extra travel time on roads including the Eastern Distributor, Moore Park Rd, Cleveland St, Anzac Pde and Lang Rd.

properties.publicTransport: Match tickets include travel on public transport. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Sydney Super Cup: Chelsea F.C. v Western Sydney Wanderers at Accor Stadium on Tuesday 28 July. Gates open at 6:15pm. Game kick-off 7:45pm. Full-time 9.40pm.

With thousands of football fans expected to attend Chelsea FC v Western Sydney Wanderers, delays are expected on roads approaching the precinct.  

Motorists should allow plenty of extra travel time on roads approaching Olympic Park including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

  • PARKING: car parks are expected to be in high demand, fans are urged to pre-book parking online ahead of time to secure a space. A flat rate $40 parking fee applies. 
  • If you have pre-booked your parking, follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.
  • Carpool with your group and allow plenty of extra travel time as traffic will be heavy during the busy weeknight peak.
  • Alternatively, consider parking at a nearby train station commuter car park and continuing your trip by train to Olympic Park, at no extra cost.

Sydney Olympic Park Authority will close local roads in the precinct from 4pm to 11.30pm on Tuesday 28 July  including Olympic Boulevard from Grand Parade to Dawn Fraser Avenue, and Showground Road from Murray Rose Avenue to Dawn Fraser Avenue. For road closure details, visit the Sydney Olympic Park Authority interactive map.
properties.displayName: Chelsea FC v Western Sydney Wanderers

properties.publicTransport: Match tickets include travel on public transport. Express trains and Major Event Buses will run. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Sydney Super Cup: Chelsea F.C. v Tottenham Hotspur at Accor Stadium on Saturday 1 August. Gates open at 5:45pm. Game kick-off 7:45pm. Full-time approx. 9.40pm.

With around 70,000 people expected to attend the match, significant delays are expected on roads approaching the precinct.  

Motorists should allow plenty of extra travel time on roads approaching Olympic Park including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

  • PARKING: car parks are expected to be in high demand, fans are urged to pre-book parking online ahead of time to secure a space. A flat rate $40 parking fee applies. 
  • If you have pre-booked your parking, follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.
  • Carpool with your group and allow plenty of extra travel time as traffic will be heavy.
  • Alternatively, consider parking at a nearby train station commuter car park and continuing your trip by train to Olympic Park, at no extra cost.

Sydney Olympic Park Authority will close local roads in the precinct and traffic congestion is expected before and after the match. For road closure details, visit the Sydney Olympic Park Authority interactive map.

arrangementElements.html:

properties.publicTransport: Travel on transport is included in match tickets and additional light rail services will run. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Tottenham Hotspur v Sydney FC at Allianz Stadium on Wednesday 29 July. Kick-off at 7.45pm, full time around 9.40pm.

Traffic delays are expected on roads approaching Moore Park as football fans make their way to the precinct in the already busy weeknight peak.

Motorists should carpool and allow plenty of extra travel time on roads including the Eastern Distributor, Moore Park Rd, Cleveland St, Anzac Pde and Lang Rd.

Changed traffic conditions:

  • Driver Avenue will be partially closed outside the stadium from 4pm and will reopen once crowds have safely cleared the area.   
  • No through-traffic will be available on Driver Avenue, however access to EP3 grass car park and drop-off areas maintained from Moore Park Road and Lang Road. Car Parks open from 4.45pm. 
  • Special event clearways will be in place on roads in Moore Park and surrounding areas from 3pm to midnight on sections of Albion Street, Moore Park Road and Lang Road, and from 6am to midnight on sections of Cleveland Street and Fitzroy Street. 

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

  • Moore Park Road between Flinders Street and Greens Road (northern side)
